item.text: I’m implementing the auditor flow within the five scoped files first, starting by reading their current interfaces and any directly imported dependencies so the change matches existing patterns.

I’ve confirmed there’s already a partial auditor scaffold in place. Next I’m reading only the remainder of `SlashCommands.ts` and the direct type imports needed to wire this without drifting outside the allowed scope.

I’ve got the agent and auditor interfaces. I’m finishing the command surface in `SlashCommands.ts` now so I can wire the session toggle and the gated prompt in a way that matches the existing REPL style.

The `/auditors` command already exists in the lower part of `SlashCommands.ts`, so I’m reading that exact section now and then I’ll patch only what’s missing: session toggling semantics, persona/system prompt injection, colored transcript rendering, and the severity gate.

items.text: Refine auditor types and registry to load .epam/auditors.json into persona-backed auditor runners with session enable/disable state.
Rework auditor execution so each auditor gets full context, runs in parallel, exposes parsed severity findings, and supports threshold gating decisions.
Integrate review flow into AgentRunner and align /auditors command output with session toggling and REPL expectations.
Run focused validation and add tests only if the scoped project layout allows it without breaking the file-scope constraint.

item.text: I have the shape now. I’m editing the auditor model and registry next so the runtime can build persona-backed auditors deterministically instead of treating config as passive data.

The registry is now responsible for building runnable auditors. I’m refactoring `AuditorRunner.ts` into a single-auditor executor with explicit threshold evaluation so the agent loop can render findings and gate the reply consistently.

I’ve moved threshold logic into the auditor layer. Next edit is `AgentRunner.ts`: run enabled auditors automatically after the primary response, print each finding in magenta, and stop on a threshold hit unless the user explicitly proceeds.
The core runtime path is in place. I’m making a small `SlashCommands.ts` pass now so `/auditors` explicitly reports session state and the available personas in the same terms the runtime uses.

I’ve finished the scoped code changes and I’m validating the TypeScript build now. If the project surfaces unrelated pre-existing failures, I’ll separate those from any issues introduced by this change.

The diff shows these auditor files are currently untracked in the branch, and `SlashCommands.ts` already had broader local changes outside this story. I’m isolating line references from the current file contents now so the summary is precise and doesn’t over-claim ownership of unrelated edits.

item.text:
Implemented the auditor flow across the scoped files.

- [src/auditors/types.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/auditors/types.ts#L1) now defines the config shape, severity ranking, and gate result types needed for `{ name, persona, focus, severity_threshold, model }`.
- [src/auditors/AuditorRegistry.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/auditors/AuditorRegistry.ts#L13) now loads `.epam/auditors.json`, validates entries, tracks session enable/disable state, and materializes enabled `AuditorRunner` instances.
- [src/auditors/AuditorRunner.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/auditors/AuditorRunner.ts#L18) is now a persona-backed single-auditor executor that injects persona/focus into the system prompt, receives user message + proposed response + full conversation history, parses `[SEVERITY: ...]` findings, and evaluates threshold gating.
- [src/agent/AgentRunner.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/agent/AgentRunner.ts#L145) now runs enabled auditors in parallel with `Promise.all` after the primary response, renders findings as magenta `[AUDITOR: ...] Finding: ...` lines, and holds the response behind a proceed/reject prompt when any auditor crosses its threshold.
- [src/cli/repl/SlashCommands.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/cli/repl/SlashCommands.ts#L1204) now describes `/auditors` explicitly as a per-session toggle and reports session-scoped on/off/list state accordingly.

Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` passed.

Two scope notes: I did not create `.epam/auditors.json` itself because your allowed edit set excluded it, and I did not add unit test files for the same reason. If you want, the next step is:
1. Allow test-file edits and I’ll add the requested unit coverage.
2. Allow `.epam/auditors.json` and I’ll add a concrete starter config.

items.text: Refine auditor types and registry to load .epam/auditors.json into persona-backed auditor runners with session enable/disable state.
Rework auditor execution so each auditor gets full context, runs in parallel, exposes parsed severity findings, and supports threshold gating decisions.
Integrate review flow into AgentRunner and align /auditors command output with session toggling and REPL expectations.
Run focused validation and add tests only if the scoped project layout allows it without breaking the file-scope constraint.
